title: Cross-tabulate segment memberships across solutions
summary: Builds a single stacked cross-tabulation of segment labels across one or more solution columns in a memberships CSV. Writes a square matrix (total row/col plus one row/col per level of each solution) as a CSV sidecar and an xlsx workbook with merged solution-name headers, a 3-colour scale across the data body, and frozen header panes.
description: Given a memberships CSV with a respondent id and one or more solution columns (e.g. `sol_3`, `sol_4`), computes `table(sol_i, sol_j)` for every pair and assembles the results into a stacked matrix of size `1 + Σ k_i` where `k_i` is the number of levels in solution i. Row/column 0 is the total count and per-level marginals. Missing values are excluded. Used to see how respondents migrate between competing segmentations, or to confirm the per-level sample sizes of a single solution.
usage:
[["seg-profile::migration"]]
memberships = "<memberships.csv>"
run_dir = "./runs"
run_name = "m1"
# optional:
uuid_col = "resp_id"
solution_cols = ["sol_3", "sol_4"]

arguments.name: memberships
arguments.description: CSV with per-respondent segment labels across one or more solution columns.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run; no '..', '/' or '\\'.

arguments.name: uuid_col
arguments.description: Respondent identifier column. If omitted, the first column is treated as the id.

arguments.name: solution_cols
arguments.description: Subset of solution columns to include. Empty = use all non-uuid columns.

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether the CSV has a header row.

notes: The xlsx uses 3-colour conditional formatting (lavender → white → orange) over the body only, so the total row/col does not dominate the scale.
Diagonal blocks are self-cross-tabs (sol_i × sol_i); off-diagonal blocks are (sol_i × sol_j).

artifacts.title: Segment migration workbook
artifacts.description: A cross-tabulation matrix showing how respondents are distributed across multiple segmentation solutions. Diagonal blocks show each solution's own segment sizes; off-diagonal blocks show how respondents from one solution's segments map across another's. Used to assess stability between competing segmentations.

artifacts.title: Migration matrix data
artifacts.description: The same cross-tabulation in CSV format for further processing.
